items.claim: Max Holloway has an edge because he has been more active.
items.quote: I would have to go with Max having that edge because he's been more active.

items.claim: Conor McGregor has an edge because he has fought at 170 pounds before.
items.quote: They're fighting at 170, so that does give Conor edge because he's fought at 170 before

items.claim: Conor McGregor is not as hungry as he used to be.
items.quote: I don't think anyone can really argue that McGregor's hungry like he used to be.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's lifestyle outside the cage raises questions about his training consistency.
items.quote: I do think there's a lot of like questions about how McGregor like his lifestyle outside the cage, you know, and then being able to train

items.claim: Fighters typically experience a slow decline as they get older.
items.quote: in MMA, the older you get like it's usually a slow decline

items.claim: Conor McGregor possesses more strength and power than Max Holloway at 170 pounds.
items.quote: Conor has more strength, more power

items.claim: Max Holloway will be slower at 170 pounds because he is a big fighter.
items.quote: he will I think he will like slower down himself because his weight he's big.

items.claim: A long layoff of 4-5 years negatively impacts a fighter's performance.
items.quote: anytime someone has 4-5 years off, uh it's not great

items.claim: Conor McGregor is likely to make errors due to his current lifestyle.
items.quote: how Conor McGregor's been living his life, I think he's going to make a lot of errors.
